data.title: LPL Financial targets 90% asset retention for Commonwealth onboarding while advancing $2.4T asset base
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: LPL Financial Holdings Inc. (LPLA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Richard Steinmeier highlighted a milestone year, stating, "we delivered industry-leading organic asset growth of 8%, including the onboarding of the retail wealth management businesses of Wintrust Financial and First Horizon, which collectively support over 200 financial advisers managing roughly $34 billion in client assets." He noted the completed integration of Atria Wealth Solutions and the acquisition of Commonwealth Financial Network, adding, "we remain on track to onboard the Commonwealth advisers in Q4." Steinmeier also announced record adjusted earnings per share of $20.09 for the year.
  • Steinmeier reported, "total assets increased to a record $2.4 trillion, driven by organic growth and higher equity markets," with organic net new assets of $23 billion in Q4. He emphasized that "asset retention was 97% for Q4 and over the last 12 months."
  • Regarding Commonwealth, Steinmeier explained, "We continue to expect roughly 90% retention of client assets. As we get closer to onboarding later this year, our estimate will continue to firm up."
  • CFO Matthew Audette stated, "Total advisory and brokerage assets were $2.4 trillion, up 2% from Q3 as continued organic growth was complemented by higher equity markets." He added, "The combination of organic growth and expense discipline led to adjusted pretax margin of approximately 36% and record adjusted EPS of $5.23."

Outlook

  • Audette indicated, "In 2026, we plan to continue to invest in the business to deliver greater efficiencies and drive operating leverage as we scale. Prior to Commonwealth, we expect core G&A growth of 4.5% to 7% or $1.775 billion to $1.820 billion. In addition, we'll have the full year impact of expenses related to Commonwealth, which adds roughly $380 million to $390 million."
  • For Q1, Audette guided, "we expect first quarter service and fee revenue to increase by approximately $25 million sequentially." He also mentioned, "we expect core G&A to be in a range of $540 million to $560 million" and promotional expense "to be roughly flat sequentially."
  • On Commonwealth, Steinmeier reaffirmed, "We continue to expect roughly 90% retention of client assets" and projected the onboarding to remain on track for Q4.

Financial Results

  • Audette reported, "Gross profit was $1.542 billion, up $62 million sequentially. Commission and advisory fees net of payout were $453 million, up $27 million from Q3."
  • Client cash revenue was $456 million, up $14 million from Q3. "Overall client cash balances ended the quarter at $61 billion, up $5 billion sequentially," Audette stated.
  • The ICA yield was 341 basis points in Q4, down 10 basis points from Q3.
  • Audette said, "Core G&A was $536 million in Q4, bringing our full year core G&A to $1.852 billion, below the low end of our outlook range."
  • Audette noted, "TA loan amortization was $133 million in Q4, up $28 million sequentially, driven by Commonwealth-related transition assistance."
  • Corporate cash ended Q4 at $470 million, down $99 million from Q3. The leverage ratio was 1.95x at the end of Q4.

Q&A

  • Steven Chubak, Wolfe Research: Asked about confidence in achieving "the 90% asset retention figure" for Commonwealth and actions to return core recruiting to prior levels. Audette responded that "on average, [retained advisers] are larger, they are faster growing, and they are higher producers than those that have decided to go elsewhere." Steinmeier added, "We are very confident that by keeping the community intact, safeguarding their experience... that will ultimately win the day."
  • Alexander Blostein, Goldman Sachs: Inquired why the $425 million EBITDA estimate for Commonwealth had not increased. Audette explained, "you've got assets have gone up a bit... but you've also got another interest rate cut... so those things kind of net offset each other."
  • Daniel Fannon, Jefferies: Asked about the timeline for reigniting the growth engine and adviser recruiting. Steinmeier said, "as recruiting activity normalizes, we'd expect organic growth to pick up as those pipelines convert."
  • Craig Siegenthaler, BofA Securities: Questioned liquidity trends and use of purchase money market funds. Audette explained, "as the rate environment comes down... it's very natural that those are the types of funds that will go back into play."
  • Michael Cho, JPMorgan: Asked about Commonwealth integration progress. Steinmeier detailed capability builds and stated, "we feel really good about our ability to deliver against that capability development and for our advisers to experience it."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns around Commonwealth retention, recruiting normalization, and the impact of interest rates, with a slightly negative to neutral tone, as seen in Chubak's question about press coverage and Blostein's focus on financial targets.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, frequently using phrases such as "we are very confident" and "we feel really good about our ability to deliver." They were direct in reaffirming targets and explaining operational strategies, though they acknowledged ongoing diligence and timing uncertainties for adviser decisions.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained confident, but analysts appeared more probing and cautious regarding retention and growth pacing.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Organic net new asset growth slowed to $23 billion in Q4 from $33 billion in Q3, and the annualized growth rate declined from 7% to 4%.
  • Recruited assets for Q4 were $14 billion, down from $33 billion in Q3, indicating a shift in recruiting resources toward Commonwealth retention.
  • Asset retention remained strong at 97%, though slightly below the prior quarter's 98% (excluding offboarded assets).
  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 was $5.23, up slightly from $5.20 in Q3.
  • Core G&A rose in Q4 to $536 million from $477 million in Q3, with an outlook for further increase in 2026 as Commonwealth expenses are incorporated.
  • Management reiterated its 90% asset retention goal for Commonwealth, with signed commitments rising from nearly 80% to the low 80% range quarter-over-quarter.
  • Analysts' focus shifted further toward integration risks, recruiting normalization, and the interest rate environment, while management sustained a constructive, proactive communications stance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited continued competitive pressure in adviser recruiting, with "competitors remain aggressive" and elevated TA levels persisting.
  • Asset retention for Commonwealth remains a focal risk, though management expressed confidence in achieving 90%.
  • Analysts questioned the pace of recruiting normalization and the impact of interest rate cuts on cash and asset flows.
  • Audette discussed G&A growth guidance, noting the balancing act between efficiency gains and necessary investments in technology and platform enhancements.

Final Takeaway

LPL Financial closed 2025 with record assets and earnings amid the largest acquisition in its history, maintaining a clear focus on integrating Commonwealth Financial Network and targeting 90% asset retention when onboarding in Q4 2026. Management highlighted disciplined expense management, continued strategic investments, and a robust recruiting pipeline, while acknowledging industry competition and timing uncertainties. Despite a temporary slowdown in organic asset growth and recruiting, the company remains positioned to deliver industry-leading growth as adviser movement normalizes and integration efforts advance over the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Deckers raises FY2026 revenue outlook to up to $5.425B as HOKA and UGG drive double-digit growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Deckers Outdoor Corporation (DECK) Q3 2026

Management View

  • CEO Stefano Caroti reported strong third quarter results, highlighting global demand for both the HOKA and UGG brands and noting, "Deckers delivered an outstanding third quarter performance, underscored by a strong composition of results that demonstrate robust global demand for our brands, fueling an increased outlook for fiscal year 2026." Caroti detailed that third quarter revenue reached $1.96 billion, a 7% increase year over year, with HOKA revenue up 18% and UGG revenue up 5%. He described balanced growth across direct-to-consumer (DTC) and wholesale channels for both brands as well as international and US markets.
  • Caroti emphasized the effectiveness of strategic product allocation, especially for UGG, and noted the successful launch of new products such as the Quill franchise and the expansion of the Lowmel sneaker, which "more than doubled its revenue this quarter and ranked among the brand's top 5 best sellers."
  • The CEO also highlighted HOKA's international expansion and market share gains, stating, "HOKA's market share increased significantly in the road running category above $140 for the 3 months ending in December," and described the brand’s growing presence in Europe and Asia.
  • CFO Steve Fasching stated, "Our third quarter performance exceeded expectations and demonstrated robust momentum of the UGG and HOKA brands... These results are a testament to the exceptional strength of our premium brands within the U.S. and internationally as our disciplined approach to marketplace management, combined with innovative product and an elevated consumer experience led to high levels of full price selling and exceptional performance during the holiday season."

Outlook

  • Fasching raised full-year 2026 revenue expectations to a range of $5.4 billion to $5.425 billion, up from prior guidance. HOKA is expected to deliver mid-teens revenue growth, and UGG is now expected to increase mid-single digits, which is at the high end of previous guidance.
  • Gross margin guidance was increased to approximately 57%, 100 basis points above prior expectations, while operating margin was raised to approximately 22.5%.
  • Fasching provided an updated diluted earnings per share outlook of $6.80 to $6.85, representing a 7% to 8% increase over last year.
  • Fasching stated, "Our increased full year 2026 guidance includes the following assumptions for the fourth quarter. HOKA is expected to deliver 13% to 14% growth, representing the brand's largest ever quarterly revenue...UGG revenue is assumed to be roughly flat to last year."

Financial Results

  • Third quarter revenue was reported at $1.96 billion, a 7% increase versus the prior year.
  • Gross margin for the quarter was 59.8%, exceeding expectations due to a lower-than-expected impact from increased tariffs.
  • Diluted earnings per share reached a record $3.33, up 11% from the previous year.
  • SG&A expenses were $557 million, representing 28.5% of revenue, down from 29.3% a year ago.
  • The company ended the quarter with $2.1 billion in cash and equivalents and no outstanding borrowings. Inventory was $633 million, up 10% year over year.
  • Deckers repurchased approximately $349 million in shares in the quarter and plans to repurchase over $1 billion for the fiscal year.

Q&A

  • Jay Sole, UBS: Asked about HOKA’s acceleration and sustainability. Caroti responded, "I do see it sustainable going forward...We decided to space out key franchise launches with tightened inventories of outgoing styles, and we better leverage our DTC channel to move closeouts in a controlled manner."
  • Sole also inquired about HOKA’s lifestyle initiatives. Caroti noted the category as a "huge opportunity" and cited early positive results from new products.
  • Peter McGoldrick, Stifel: Asked about UGG’s channel strategy. Caroti stated, "You should continue to see a balanced growth in the UGG portfolio...continued segmentation of the marketplace, continued differentiation and growth across all channels, markets and categories."
  • Laurent Vasilescu, BNP Paribas: Inquired about HOKA's Q4 guidance and order book. Fasching explained the approach to managing sustainable growth and noted, "We're anticipating growth for UGG in FY '27...We're very encouraged by how the order book is developing globally."
  • Paul Lejuez, Citi: Asked about HOKA wholesale performance. Caroti responded that "no major changes" were seen and that sell-through outpaced sell-in across all channels.
  • Rakesh Patel, Raymond James: Sought clarity on HOKA DTC inflection. Caroti attributed it to the HOKA membership program and a cleaner marketplace.
  • Dana Telsey, Telsey Advisory: Asked about DTC channel differences and wholesale partnerships. Caroti reported strong performance across both retail and e-commerce, with success in major retailers and run specialty channels.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on sustainability of HOKA's growth trajectory, channel strategy, and the durability of DTC improvements, with a generally positive but probing tone. There was particular interest in the balance between wholesale and DTC and the ability to sustain growth amid macroeconomic uncertainty.
  • Management’s tone was confident, underscored by repeated references to "robust demand," "exceptional performance," and "high levels of full price selling." During the Q&A, management provided detailed responses without deflection, and Caroti stated, "I feel confident that this trajectory will continue."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts showed increased confidence, with management raising guidance and analysts focusing more on growth execution and less on consumer caution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance was raised in the current quarter for revenue, gross margin, and EPS, whereas the previous quarter had reflected a more cautious outlook due to macroeconomic and tariff-related headwinds.
  • Management’s confidence increased, shifting from caution about the U.S. consumer to optimism following a strong holiday season and improved demand visibility.
  • Analysts’ questions moved from concerns over consumer headwinds and tariff impacts to focus on growth drivers, channel execution, and long-term opportunities, especially for HOKA.
  • Both quarters highlighted international strength, but the current quarter gave more detail on successful product launches and membership programs driving DTC growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing tariff impacts, with Fasching stating, "we now expect the unmitigated tariff impact on fiscal year 2026 to be approximately $110 million," though this was partially offset by price actions and inventory timing.
  • There is an expected "200 basis point headwind" to gross margin in Q4 due to tariffs.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of growth rates, DTC inflection, and potential fluctuations in channel performance. Management reaffirmed their commitment to balanced and sustainable growth across all channels and geographies.

Final Takeaway

Deckers management emphasized outstanding third quarter results and raised full-year 2026 guidance, citing robust demand for HOKA and UGG globally. Both brands achieved balanced growth across DTC and wholesale channels, with strong product innovation and effective marketplace management. The company expects continued momentum in the fourth quarter and into fiscal 2027, supported by strong brand positioning, an expanded product pipeline, and disciplined operational execution, while remaining vigilant regarding tariff impacts and macroeconomic uncertainties.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Crude oil climbs to highest since summer on growing bets of U.S. strike on Iran
data.description:
Crude oil futures gained for a third straight session Thursday, with Brent crude rising back above $70/bbl, as U.S. military moves into the Middle East raised expectations of possible action against Iran.

The U.S. has moved more firepower into the region, and President Trump has urged Iran to reach a nuclear deal or face severe consequences, and reports say the president wants to create conditions for "regime change" after the crackdown that crushed ‌a nationwide protest movement earlier this month, killing thousands of people.

The Associated Press reported Iran issued a warning to ships at sea that it planned to run a drill next week that would include live firing in the Strait of Hormuz, sparking worries of a potential closure of the critical chokepoint through which 20% of the world's oil flows.

Iran pumps ~3.3M bbl/day of oil and exports ~1.5M bbl/day, and 20M bbl/day passes through the Strait of Hormuz, meaning any supply disruption could have far-reaching consequences.

Prices also were supported by an unexpected draw in U.S. crude inventories last week and a weaker dollar that helps boost demand for commodities.

Oil markets are repricing geopolitical risk on the rising probability of direct U.S. action against Iran, Rystad Energy's Jorge Leon said in a note, and "markets appear to be taking [Trump's] statements seriously, particularly given the administration's track record of following through on military threats, in contrast to its trade-related rhetoric, which has often been scaled back or delayed."

"While we continue to believe an oil supply imbalance is developing, geopolitical risks are likely to lift prices further," Peter Cardillo of Spartan Capital said in a note, with further gains likely to be met with increasing short positioning. "In other words, short the rallies."

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for March delivery finished +3.5% to $65.42/bbl, its best settlement value since September 26, and front-month Brent March crude (CO1:COM) ended +3.4% to $70.71/bbl, its highest close since July 31.

U.S. natural gas futures rose for the eighth consecutive session, as frigid temperatures are expected to continue driving very strong national heating demand through the weekend; front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for March delivery settled +5% at $3.918/MMBtu.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.title: Chinalco, Rio Tinto near deal to purchase Brazil's CBA - Bloomberg
data.description:
Aluminum Corp. of China is close to announcing a deal to acquire controlling ownership of Brazilian aluminum producer Companhia Brasileira de Aluminio, in a deal that would give Rio Tinto (RIO) a one-third stake, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

Chinalco would buy control of CBA from Brazil's Grupo Votorantim and then propose acquiring stakes held by minority investors in a public offering, and Rio Tinto (RIO) would hold 33% of the new joint venture, according to the report.

Grupo Votorantim was said to be trying to find a partner for a $2.5B investment in CBA’s Projeto Rondon to explore a bauxite mine in the Amazon, and Chinalco, along with other suitors, ended up showing interest in acquiring control of CBA.

CBA had a market value of ~6.7B reais ($1.2B) at the close of trading in Brazil, with shares priced at 10.35 reais; Chinalco and Rio Tinto (RIO) reportedly would pay 10.50 reais/share in the deal.

data.title: Paramount Gold soars after winning U.S. approval for Grassy Mountain gold project
data.description:
Paramount Gold Nevada (PZG) +37.5% post-market Thursday after saying it was issued a record of decision from the U.S. Bureau of Land Management for its Grassy Mountain gold project in Oregon, culminating the federal environmental review process under the National Environmental Policy Act.

The ROD and Final Environmental Impact Statement, which incorporates feedback received during the public comment period, will be available on the BLM’s permitting dashboard through the e-planning and FAST-41 portals, the company said.

"We welcome the Record of Decision as a historic milestone for both Grassy Mountain and the state of Oregon," Paramount Gold (PZG) CEO Rachel Goldman said. "With this approval, Grassy Mountain becomes a shovel-ready project that will contribute to the reinvigorated U.S. mining industry."

data.title: Newbridge Acquisition Limited announces pricing of $50 million initial public offering
data.description:
• Newbridge Acquisition Limited (NBRG) announced on Thursday that it priced its initial public offering of 5,000,000 units at $10.00 per unit.
  •  The units are expected to be listed on the NASDAQ Capital Market  and trade under the ticker symbol "NBRGU" beginning January 30, 2026.
  • Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one right. Each right entitles the holder thereof to receive one-eighth (1/8) of one Class A ordinary share upon the consummation of an initial business combination. As a result, eight rights are needed to receive one Class A ordinary share at the closing of the initial business combination.
  • Once the securities comprising the units begin separate trading, the Class A ordinary shares and rights are expected to be listed on NASDAQ under the symbols "NBRG" and "NBRGR," respectively.
  • The underwriters have been granted a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 750,000 units offered by the  company to cover over-allotments, if any.
  • The offering is expected to close on February 2, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CVR Energy prices $1 billion private placement of senior notes
data.description:
• CVR Energy (CVI) on Thursday said it priced a private placement of $600 million of 7.500% senior unsecured notes due 2031 and $400 million of 7.875% senior unsecured notes due 2034.
  • The notes will be issued at par and are guaranteed on a senior unsecured basis by certain of the company’s domestic subsidiaries.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds, along with cash on hand and borrowings under its asset-based revolving credit facility, to repay its senior secured term loan, redeem all outstanding 8.500% senior notes due 2029, and redeem $217 million of 5.750% senior notes due 2028.
  • The offering is expected to close on February 12, 2026, subject to customary conditions.
  • The notes have not been registered under U.S. securities laws and will be offered only to qualified institutional buyers and non-U.S. investors.
  • CVI +0.31% after hours to $22.35.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SL Green signals 2026 occupancy target of 94.8% as leasing momentum accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: SL Green Realty Corp. (SLG) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Marc Holliday, Interim President, Chairman & CEO, expressed strong confidence in New York City's fiscal stability and the commercial office sector's prospects, noting, "I think 2026 is setting up to be quite an amazing year for the commercial office sector in terms of occupancy gains, rental achievement and business growth." He highlighted robust business activity, citing "the big 5 banks just reported increases to earnings year-over-year with profits in the fourth quarter up 6.7%, and investment banking revenues up 12.6%." Holliday detailed a recent 10-day trip to Asia, reporting unprecedented global appetite for New York real estate: "The appetite to invest in New York was as strong as I have ever seen."

  • Holliday outlined ambitious plans for 2026, including a $7 billion refinancing initiative and a $2.5 billion asset disposition program, emphasizing, "We set lofty goals for ourselves in December, as we always do, and know you all will be monitoring our progress every step of the way, as you should."

  • Harrison Sitomer, Chief Investment Officer, shared that "we have seen a continued tightening of senior loans as demonstrated by our recent financing of Park Avenue Tower," and announced the launch of fundraising for a new fund focused on senior credit lending. Sitomer highlighted, "We are in negotiations on contracts and term sheets on 4 additional transactions in our $2.5 billion plan."

  • Matthew Diliberto, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "From an earnings perspective, we printed an FFO beat of $0.02 a share, driven by higher NOI due to lower expenses, net of reimbursements, which came through both in the earnings beat and in same-store cash NOI that was better than we expected for the quarter." Diliberto added, "We ended the year at 93% [occupancy], which is sector-leading and reflects an increase of almost 400 basis points since the lows at the end of the first quarter of 2024."

Outlook

  • Diliberto reiterated confidence in the company's leasing trajectory, setting a 2026 same-store occupancy objective of 94.8% by year-end. Diliberto projected, "It translates into significant same-store NOI growth, 3.5% to 4.5% over the course of the year." Management indicated that a strong leasing pipeline and ongoing asset sales will support these targets.

  • No explicit comparison to analysts' estimates was provided due to a lack of sufficient data.

Financial Results

  • Diliberto highlighted an FFO beat of $0.02 per share for the fourth quarter, attributing the outperformance to higher NOI, improved hospitality business contributions, and lower G&A. He noted that the company "actually beat the initial guidance we gave back in December of 2024 by $65 million, almost $20 million of which happened in the fourth quarter alone."

  • The company closed nearly 800,000 square feet of Manhattan office leasing in the quarter, totaling 2.6 million square feet for the year, and achieved sector-leading occupancy. Diliberto stated, "Including the same-store leases that were signed after January 1 in our December occupancy, we would have been at 93.2%."

Q&A

  • Alexander Goldfarb, Piper Sandler: Asked about AI's impact on tenant demand. Steven Durels, Executive VP, responded that "I've not heard of a single instance of the deals that we've done where tenants have downsized as a result of AI. Just the opposite, many of the deals that we're working on... have some element of growth."

  • John Kim, BMO: Inquired about the timing of recognizing $78 million in rental revenue from economic vs. physical occupancy. Diliberto explained, "How it bleeds in somewhat out of our control because the tenants control when they finish their space and can move in, and that's what triggers revenue recognition."

  • Nicholas Yulico, Scotiabank: Asked about asset sales timing and dividend policy. Diliberto clarified most asset sales will impact the back half of the year, spanning "stabilized office, development sites, residential, and retail." On dividends, Holliday said, "We don't peg the policy quarter-to-quarter. It's intended to be underpinning of a long-term plan of investment, and harvesting, repatriation and creating free cash flow."

  • Anthony Paolone, JPMorgan: Queried about economic occupancy guidance and Worldwide Plaza's FFO impact. Diliberto confirmed year-end economic occupancy is expected to be higher and reported, "It generates $7 million of FFO."

  • Several analysts probed leasing pipeline quality, concessions trends, and capital deployment from the debt fund. Management described a strong leasing pipeline, stable concessions, and fund deployment targeting mid-teens returns.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on the sustainability of earnings, timing of asset sales, dividend policy, and leasing economics, displaying a slightly skeptical to neutral tone, particularly regarding recurring profitability and dividend sustainability.

  • Management maintained an optimistic and confident tone throughout, frequently citing "a very high degree of confidence in executing on that plan" and repeatedly emphasizing sector leadership and growth prospects. During Q&A, management remained constructive but deflected on quarterly dividend specifics and timing of certain revenue streams, indicating a preference for long-term context.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the current call featured a more assertive and bullish management tone, with analysts maintaining measured skepticism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from projecting occupancy at 93.2% year-end in Q3 to a new target of 94.8% by the end of 2026.

  • Strategic focus in Q4 centered on executing a $7 billion refinancing and $2.5 billion disposition plan, compared to Q3’s emphasis on acquisitions and new development sites.

  • Analysts in both quarters focused on asset sales, leasing momentum, dividend sustainability, and profitability metrics, but Q4 included more granular discussion of fee income, leasing pipeline composition, and international capital flows.

  • Management’s tone in Q4 was more confident, highlighting sector-leading occupancy gains and an improved capital markets environment, while Q3 focused more on navigating challenges and recent transaction activity.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the unpredictability of FAD due to tenant capital spend and timing of asset sales, noting, "FAD is not the governor of dividend."

  • Analysts raised concerns about dividend sustainability, timing of asset sales, and the impact of delayed revenue recognition due to the difference between economic and physical occupancy.

  • Management emphasized a diversified revenue strategy, stating the dividend policy is based on "an accumulation of taxable income items," not just FAD or FFO metrics.

Final Takeaway

SL Green management reiterated optimism for 2026, underpinned by strong leasing activity, robust demand from both domestic and international investors, and sector-leading occupancy gains. The company aims to drive NOI growth and execute its refinancing and disposition plans, positioning itself for continued earnings momentum. Management stressed confidence in the city’s business climate and in their ability to deliver above-peer performance, emphasizing that the portfolio is at its highest earning capacity and poised for further opportunity and value growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gold and silver end higher, but sudden midday selloff may indicate near-term peak
data.description:
Gold and silver futures settled slightly higher in a volatile session after gold retreated suddenly in late-morning trading from a record above $5,600/oz and silver slipped from a record $120/oz.

"We believe this reversal is significant," and it may indicate that precious metals prices have found a near-term high, Spartan Capital analyst Peter Cardillo said in a note.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for February delivery closed +0.3% to $5,318.40/oz, pulling back from an intraday record above $5,600/oz but nevertheless setting another new all-time high, and front-month February Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +0.7% at $114.037/oz, its second-highest settlement in history.

Both metals started much higher in early trading on a weaker dollar, threats of U.S. military strikes on Iran, and a looming deadline for the U.S. government to avoid another shutdown.

Despite the day's sudden pullback, UBS analysts led by Giovanni Staunovo said gold remains an attractive asset and a strong hedge, forecasting the metal to reach $6,200 for the first three quarters of the year, driven by higher investment activity.

The World Gold Council said it expects global demand for gold to remain strong this year, driven by lower interest rates, uncertainty in bond markets, and persistent geopolitical risks.

"Gold's appeal as an all-weather hedge relative to fixed income should continue to attract material investor demand into 2026, and possibly beyond," WGC said in its quarterly report.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)
